query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
**Summary: No new Uganda cases after 21 June 2026 — Uganda remains at 20 confirmed cases and has NOT exceeded 20.**

- **WHO DON 2026-DON612** (published 3 July 2026): Uganda = 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths, 1 probable (deceased), 16 recoveries. The last confirmed Uganda case was identified on **21 June 2026**.
- **ECDC** outbreak page (last updated 10 July 2026) explicitly states: "The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then." **CDTR Week 27** (27 Jun–3 Jul) showed no new Uganda cases.
- Uganda cases = 15 imported + 5 secondary; confined to Kampala/Wakiso; no community transmission.
- Broader outbreak (DRC-driven) continues to grow: DRC ~1,792 confirmed / ~625 deaths as of 8 July 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (WHO DON 2026-DON612)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, plus one probable case who has died. The last confirmed case was identified on 21 June 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: Last updated 10 July 2026. Uganda: 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths, 16 recovered. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then. CDTR Week 27 referenced.

sources.title: Disease Outbreak News: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (3 July 2026)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, as well as one probable case who has died.

sources.title: Bundibugyo virus disease updates - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 8 July 2026, health authorities had reported 1792 laboratory-confirmed cases (including 625 deaths) in DRC across Ituri, North Kivu and South Kivu.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: 625 confirmed deaths in DRC and two deaths in Uganda as of the second week of July. PHEIC declared 16 May 2026; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus.

data_points.metric: Total outbreak confirmed cases (DRC + Uganda + France)
